_note: chrome-devtools is a real stdio MCP server bundled with this plugin for the /design-check QA step (screenshots, Lighthouse, console/network inspection at mobile breakpoints). Everything in _expectedConnectors below is a managed/remote Cowork connector the user enables in Settings -> Capabilities, not a stdio server this plugin can launch — documented here for discovery. No tokens are stored in this file.

_expectedConnectors.fallback: Notion REST API v1 via scripts/notion_fetch.py and scripts/notion_update.py with a user-supplied NOTION_TOKEN env var.

_expectedConnectors.note: Referenced by capability, not by a session-specific connector id. Used for verifying AssetLink metafield namespaces/keys and product/collection data; destructive operations require explicit user approval.

Cowork-managed Cloudinary Asset Management connector — primary tool for finding/uploading/organizing media in marketing/, products/, pages/ (and ad-hoc folders like 'hairloss'). Search and list operations need no API key. Treat delete-* and move-folder as destructive: confirm with the user before calling.

Main Cloudinary connector/skills (cloudinary:cloudinary-docs, cloudinary:cloudinary-transformations) for building and debugging delivery-URL transformations (f_auto, q_auto, t_hsc_* named transforms). Use alongside cloudinary-asset-management and scripts/cloudinary_url.py. The AssetLink API key lives in /Users/vMac/.env and is only needed for raw Admin API calls outside these MCP tools.
